items.title: Natvis Comes to Linux and macOS: Visualize Your C++ Types Without Writing a Single Data Formatter
items.contentSnippet: If you’ve ever debugged your own C++ containers, strings, or hash tables on Linux or macOS, you know the deal: to see anything useful in the debugger, you have to write a custom data formatter. And writing a good data formatter is genuinely painful.
items.creator: Sasha Korepanov

items.title: Your AI Agent Keeps Missing The Real Bottleneck.  JetBrains Rider Can Fix It Now.
items.contentSnippet: Here’s a case worth pondering: your app freezes for ten seconds, and you ask an AI agent what’s wrong. What does it actually do? For a long time the honest answer was: it rummages through your code and takes a wild guess. A snapshot taken by a profil
items.creator: Sasha Ivanova

items.title: Your JetBrains IDE Expertise, Now on LinkedIn

items.creator: Ekaterina Ryabukha

items.title: dotInsights | June 2026
items.contentSnippet: Did you know? The var keyword isn’t a keyword! It’s one of several “contextual” keywords in C#, and it only has special meaning when used to declare a variable. Try defining a class called var and see what happens to the rest of your codebase… Welcom
items.creator: Matt Ellis

items.title: WPF Hot Reload Is Here: Edit Your XAML and Watch It Update Live in Rider

items.title: Deprecating dotMemory Unit

items.creator: Alexander Kurakin

items.title: What Happens When You Give AI Agents the Map of Your Code’s Coverage?
items.contentSnippet: When you ask an AI agent to write a new feature, a good agent will eventually say: “I need to write a test for this.” But what happens next is usually messy. To figure out where that new test belongs, the agent has to start searching through your pro
items.creator: Sasha Ivanova

items.title: dotInsights | May 2026
items.contentSnippet: Did you know? C# supports ref return and ref local, which let you return and work with references to variables instead of copies of their values.  Welcome to dotInsights by JetBrains! This newsletter is the home for recent .NET and software developme
items.creator: Rachel Appel
